boundary_binding.action_boundary: May quality release the clinical batch?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: No exact match on batch ID between closure and target batch
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: STD-XOR-078-P mandates deviation closure must match batch X-78 exactly; CLOSE-XOR-078 covers batch X-79, creating open boundary
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: Closure covers X-79 not X-78; visible deviation alert DEV-XOR-078 remains unresolved for target batch
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: Exact match on batch, assay, criterion, and date required before release per policy STD-XOR-078-P

final_answer: The clinical batch release cannot proceed. The deviation closure (CLOSE-XOR-078) pertains to batch X-79, while the target batch for release (BATCH-XOR-078) is X-78. Policy STD-XOR-078-P requires an exact match on batch, assay, criterion, and release date before release. The closure matches assay AX-22, criterion Cq<=31, and date 2026-06-28, but the batch numbers differ. The visible deviation alert (DEV-XOR-078) confirms an open anomaly for the target batch. The source boundary remains open and the action must be escalated.
open_blockers: Batch mismatch: closure covers X-79, target is X-78
Visible deviation alert DEV-XOR-078 for target batch remains unresolved
